name: Blue Quartz Winery (Harvest Host)
summary_text: A postcard you can camp in, with peaceful overnight parking, gorgeous 360-degree mountain views, wine, craft beer, food, and incredible stargazing.
address: 2585 S F T Valley Rd, Etlan, VA 22719

siteFeatures.level: The overnight parking area is a large grassy field with enough room to find a level spot.
otherNotes: The camping area is in the middle of an open grassy field with 360-degree views.
The site was level enough to remain hitched to the truck.
Minimal tree cover provides excellent Starlink reception.
A movable picnic table and firepit were available in the general camping area.
Generator use was permitted during the visit.

food: Food Available at the Winery and Brewery When Open

reviews.text: I had an amazing stay at Blue Quartz Winery through the Harvest Hosts program. Thankfully, I was able to use the generator—a must in the hot Virginia summer! There was potable water available to fill the tank if needed. Although the winery was closed during my visit because they are closed Tuesdays and Wednesdays, Chris went above and beyond by greeting me and kindly offering wine samples before I purchased a bottle. During open hours, visitors will find plenty of indoor and outdoor seating, wine, craft beer from the brewery on site, and a kitchen serving a delicious-looking menu. The camping area is in the middle of an open field with no shade, but it was very flat. I did not need to unhitch from the truck, which was a huge bonus. Starlink reception was perfect, and a picnic table and firepit were provided in the general area and could likely be moved closer to the campsite. I had a peaceful evening stargazing, which was such a treat. The next morning, I walked around the vineyard and enjoyed the small pond nearby. The property is very pet-friendly, and my dogs had as much fun as I did. The views were beautiful, and the wine was fantastic. I will definitely be back!
